title: Wedelia subpetiolata
taxonomy: Wedelia subpetiolata belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family comprises over 23,000 species of flowering plants. Wedelia subpetiolata is a species of flowering plant in the Wedelia genus, which is native to tropical regions. The Wedelia genus is closely related to the Calceolaria genus and is part of the Heliantheae tribe. The Asteraceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world.
uses: Wedelia subpetiolata is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the regions where it is native. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. The leaves and flowers of Wedelia subpetiolata are often used in herbal remedies, and its extracts have been studied for their potential therapeutic benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses and potential applications of Wedelia subpetiolata.

preparations: Wedelia subpetiolata is used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Wedelia subpetiolata, also known as Mexican daisy or yellow heart, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Americas and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia subpetiolata include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Wedelia subpetiolata is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and gastrointestinal issues.
